description: Standardized point-in-time authority score for the company based on the HITS algorithm, measuring the impact and quality of patents within the citation network
Standardized score based on the total number of patent claims made by the firm in the last 3 years, reflecting patent scope

Standardized point-in-time score measuring how widely the company's patents are distributed across different technological classes and subclasses, with higher scores indicating greater technological diversity
Standardized point-in-time score reflecting the number of patents granted to the company in the past 3 years with foreign priority filings, signifying the firm's international innovation presence
Standardized point-in-time score reflecting the total number of patents granted to the company in the past 3 years, adjusted for industry and size
Point-in-time score aggregating the past 1 month's stock returns of firms connected to the company through patent citation links
Point-in-time score aggregating the past 6 months' stock returns of firms connected to the company through patent citation links

Point-in-time score reflecting the company's history of negative legal events related to patent maintenance over the past 3 years, with higher values indicating fewer negative events and higher patent quality
Standardized point-in-time score based on the number of unique patent classes the company has patents in over the last 3 years, indicating technological uniqueness and originality of the portfolio
Standardized point-in-time centrality score for the company in the global patent citation network using the PageRank algorithm, measuring technological importance and innovation leadership
Standardized point-in-time score based on the total number of unique external assignees whose patents cite the company's patents (inward citations), reflecting external recognition and influence
Standardized point-in-time score representing the number of unique inward citations per patent, reflecting citation quality relative to patent volume
Standardized point-in-time score based on the total number of unique external assignees that the company's patents cite (outward citations), reflecting the breadth of knowledge sources referenced
Point-in-time market capitalization of the company in US dollars
Point-in-time Patent Innovation Alpha (PIA) score for the company; a machine-learning-based indicator, percentile ranked, where higher scores predict greater expected future returns due to innovation
Point-in-time score based on the ratio of the company's reported research and development expenditure to its sales revenue, representing investment intensity in innovation
Standardized point-in-time score based on the total number of patents the company has self-filed (not acquired or licensed) in the past 3 years
